Out of This World Collection
Travel beyond the stars with this 10-piece collection inspired by the wonders of the cosmos. Eight new multichrome magnetic polishes take center stage, each specially designed to shine with velvet magnets. In other magnetic styles, like the cat eye, their deep bases reveal small color-shifting flakes and hints of sparkle, creating the look of miniature celestial creations dancing across your fingertips. Want even more cosmic sparkle? Complete the look with two star-and-moon-filled glitter toppers in gold and silver. The Out of This World Collection will be available at Polish & Beauty Expo. Prices range from $12–13 for a 15 mL bottle.

“Did I do that?” – Family Matters – Not even intended to be a main character in Family Matters, the Winslows' neighbor, Urkel, quickly stole the show with his quirky outfits, suspenders, and catchphrase, "Did I do that?" Set in Chicago, the show became part of our TGIF viewing. We laughed, wondered if Steve would ever win over Laura, and gasped when Stefan came to be. It's no wonder that Urkel quickly became a main character.
"Did I do that?" is a flake-filled jelly nail polish inspired by those fashion faux pas outfits. A deep, indigo-leaning purple base sets off bold flakes that shift from red to nearly orange, with a hint of fuchsia. Indigo flakes pop through, along with hints of royal blue and purple. This jelly shade covers well in two coats, making it a fashion do, not a fashion don't.

"You set the tone" – ER – Each week, we stepped into the ER at County General Hospital in Chicago. There, we became part of the lives of the doctors, nurses, and staff as they worked to treat the patients who came through those ER doors. Those doctors in mint scrubs kept us intrigued for 15 seasons. We watched Carter grow up, Doug and Carol navigate their relationship, and I don't know many people who can listen to "Over the Rainbow" by Israel Kamakawiwoʻole without thinking of Mark Greene.
"You Set the Tone" nail polish is a nod to those scrubs. A minty green shade with slight blue undertones, this polish features a shifty shimmer. In bright light, a strong gold shimmer shines throughout the polish. In indirect light, it shows off yellow and green, and at extreme angles, even aqua hues. Loaded with shimmer, this polish covers most nail lengths well in two coats, though longer nails may prefer three. Allow ample time for each coat to dry for the best results.

"Hi honey, did you miss me?" – Married with Children offers us a glimpse of life in the Bundys' suburban home just outside Chicago. There, we meet Al, a misogynistic shoe salesman; his wife, Peggy, a housewife with a unique sense of style; and their two children. Through banter and quick wit, this comedy kept us laughing week after week.
"Hi Honey, Did You Miss Me?" is a lavender magnetic nail polish inspired by Peggy's fashion choices. This purple magnetic polish glistens with hints of silver for a metallic feel. When worn in a cat-eye style, a vibrant blue shimmer pops against the dark background, revealing indigo tones at various angles. Worn in a velvet style, the lavender takes center stage with flashes of the blue shimmer. Two to three coats alone, or one to two coats over a black base, are recommended.

"D'oh!" – The Simpsons – Homer, Bart, Maggie, and Lisa make up one of the most memorable TV families. From the show's opening, where we see how the Simpsons end up on the couch, to Marge's blue hair, the characters' yellow skin, and even the pink walls of the family room; the traits of The Simpsons have become as familiar to us as Homer's catchphrase, "D'oh!"
"D'oh!" is a blue reflective nail polish with a strong flash-reactive blue hue. This vibrant shade is accented by a pink color-shifting shimmer that shows off fuchsia, peach, and gold tones. This flash-reactive shade covers well in two coats. For a glass-like finish after polishing, use KBShimmer’s Smooth Moves followed by Clearly On Top.

name: Clionadh Cosmetics
bio: Clionadh Cosmetics is a vegan and cruelty-free indie brand that creates handmade, high-quality beauty products. Based out of Toronto, Canada, Clionadh is co-owned and operated by sisters Leigh & Maggie.
airtableInfo:
Each year, we look at ways we can improve upon the last with customer experience being the forefront of our thoughts. This year, we have split our booth into two side-by-side. One will be for our eyeshadows only, and the other for polishes. This will allow for more shadow swatch stations while simultaneously not “clogging up the line” for those who only wish to see polishes. Our order form will have ALL of our offered products on it. When you enter a line, we can hand you a form. You are free to fill it out (with your name as well) and hand it to anyone behind our booth. Sometimes we have individuals wait in line until they reach checkout to hand in the form. We ask that you give it as soon as you are finished so we can usher you to the cash and make room for other customers at the table. From our experience, this is the best way for us to prepare your purchase as fast as possible and maintain a streamlined order. If you are at one table, and wish to purchase items from the other, you are free to do so. No need to wait in two lines unless you physically want to see each side (we know many of you come prepared with lists ahead of time!). I hope this all makes sense, but please let me know if you have any further questions.
Lastly, we are finalizing the inventory to bring. Please drop your requests below if you would like to see certain items available for purchase. With such a large catalog, it is difficult for us to narrow it down, so your feedback is very valuable! And yes, our new Halation Holos will be available. 

Clionadh Cosmetics is excited to bring you our new lineup of  Halation Holos  to Polish and Beauty Expo! We will have the entire collection with us, however we recommend coming early if you're wanting to get all eight.
Swatches below, you can also check out the YouTube video on our channel: https://youtu.be/P0-a3Bcftr8?si=CGCrKyaen2nxC8Ik

name: Glisten & Glow
bio:
Jill Rehm, a nail technician since 1996, pioneered the indie nail world by founding Glisten & Glow in 2010. Based in Bristol, CT, Glisten & Glow has earned recognition for its award-winning nail care essentials, including top coats, base coats, cuticle oils, cuticle remover, and handmade polishes.

Jill is known for creating striking crème polish collections and dazzling special-effect shades featuring flakes, shimmers, holographics, and glitters. Her inspiration comes from a wide variety of sources—art, music, photography, nature, TV, movies, travel, and her decades of experience as a nail tech—resulting in truly unique and memorable products.

With a commitment to quality, creativity, and innovation, Jill and Glisten & Glow continue to elevate the nail industry one polish at a time.

name: Hearts and Promises
bio: Hearts & Promises is a Pennsylvania based indie nail polish brand. The brand originally was known as Turtle Tootsie Polishes in 2015 but wanted a more sophisticated look and rebranded in 2018. It’s been a long time since we’ve done a polish convention and we are so excited to be accepted this year into PBE. Along with polish, we also create unique soaps, lip balms and scrubs which may be coming with us as well.
airtableInfo:
Set of 3 Chicago limited edition-

I decided to create a Christmas in July collection, including four traditional linear hollows with flakes and four more bold colors for an eight piece holiday collection. For my door buster you can get all eight polishes for just $75 while supplies last I’ll have them already wrapped in bubble wrap in the box and ready to go that you can just grab the box and throw it in your suitcase or pack it in your shipping box! There will only be 30 of these available and since it’s my first year, I don’t know if that’s a lot or if that’s not many. And don’t forget if you’re one of the first 100 customers you get a free bag of Hershey kisses as well.  so don’t forget to stop by the Hearts & Promises booth!
***These will also be available individually for $12 each or 3 or more for $10 each***

Anyway, I have a few chocolate oriented deals for you as well as free chocolate with purchase to share with you.
First, just to clarify, we are driving in from Pennsylvania. We actually live about an hour from Hershey PA. The chocolate was picked up from the factory special order and let me tell you it tastes so good! They will be in a special cooler to help with melting hopefully. That being said these are the deals I’m offering:
1. First is a three piece chocolate trio consisting of white chocolate, dark chocolate and milk chocolate colored nail polish. These are priced at $12 each or you can buy the set for $30.
2. I also have a chocolate scented oatmeal, Shea butter soap that smells so good as some swatchers will be able to describe to you in their videos. Most of us agree that it smells kind of like hot chocolate. These soaps are going to be priced at eight dollars or three for 20 and you also can purchase it for five dollars with the full trio for a total of $35.
3. Along with buying the full trio, you will have your choice of a free candy bar either Hershey’s special dark, Hershey’s milk chocolate, or cookies and cream.
4. Also, the first 100 customers to make a purchase will receive a small bag of Hershey kisses.
5. We also have a new item that just arrived, our chocolate scented brand name, heart, air fresheners. These will be priced at $3 each or 2 for $5.

Each bottle of Peculiar Polish is a symbol of strength and individuality, empowering wearers to live boldly and celebrate their one-of-a-kind selves through vibrant colors and high-quality lacquer.

This will be my 4th PBE, and I’m excited to see everyone again this year and share my creations with you!

airtableInfo:
Field Guide to Lesser-Known Cryptids - Exclusive to PBE
These are all 15ml polishes and they will each be $10 with the exception of Loveland Frog which will be $13. Each polish also includes a card depicting the inspirational cryptid!


Fresno Nightcrawler - Light grey crelly with ghostly silver flakes and navy micro holo glitter. Both solar and glow-in-the-dark; this polish gets a faint blue tint in the sun and glows bright blue in the dark. Perhaps one of the strangest cryptids ever captured on video, the Fresno Nightcrawler appears as a pair of walking legs wandering through the night. Its simple appearance has made it one of the internet's most enduring mysteries.

Enfield Horror - Aquamarine jelly base with green to gold flakes and pink shifting flakes. The Enfield Horror terrorized a small Illinois town in 1973. Witnesses described a bizarre creature with three legs, glowing eyes, and an otherworldly scream unlike anything they'd ever heard.

Hopkinsville Goblin - Lime green jelly with green shifting flakes. First reported in Kentucky in 1955, the Hopkinsville Goblin is described as a small, glowing creature with oversized eyes and long arms. Whether alien visitor or local legend, its mysterious appearance continues to fascinate cryptid enthusiasts.

Beast of Busco - Shimmering metallic multichrome that shifts from a warm copper to a reddish bronze/ The Beast of Busco is a legendary giant snapping turtle said to inhabit a lake in Indiana. Despite numerous searches, the enormous turtle has never been found, earning its place in crypid lore.

Hodag - Periwinkle to purple crelly duochrome with pink shimmer. Born from Wisconsin folklore, the Hodag is a fearsome beast said to have horns, spikes, and a reptilian tail. Through likely a tall tale, it remains an enduring symbol of Northwoods legend.

Loveland Frog - Multichrome magnetic polish in a teal base that shifts from bronze to purple and pink using the velvet magnetic technique; when magnetized using cateye, a teal, purple, and pink line forms over a deep teal to purple base. Sightings of the Loveland Frog date back to the 1950s in Ohio. Witnesses claimed to encounter a humanoid frog standing upright, sparking decades of speculation about what might be lurking along the riverbanks.

"Scenes from PBE" is a collection of 5ml polishes that will be $6 each, or $20 for all 4!

Checked out the Swatches- A rich purple crelly packed with vibrant pink shimmer. Some events require a budget. PBE is not one of them. You can't put a price on happiness, or on that one polish you absolutely had to have.

Bought All the Polishes-A soft pink crelly kissed with an ethereal blue shimmer. One of the best parts of PBE is making new polish friends who understand the excitement of discovering, collecting, and sharing beautiful nail polish.

Stood In Line- A light forest green jelly illuminated by glowing gold shimmer and pink highlights. You told yourself you were just looking, but then the shimmer caught your eye.

Made New Friends- A blue-leaning periwinkle crelly with a soft pink shimmer. Waiting in line to enter PBE is the perfect time to admire swatches, compare wish lists, and make a game plan for visiting all your favorite makers.
